channel.title: EMERGENZ Biosecurity Intel Dashboard
channel.description: High-severity biosecurity signals and recent authority/media coverage. Generated 2026-05-23T01:07:20.254Z.

item.title: [ACTION] Ebola disease — Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo
item.description: Viral hemorrhagic fever monitoring remains focused on Ebola disease activity in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and neighbouring-region readiness. Verify any current Bundibugyo-specific case counts, dates, and affected health zones directly against the latest WHO Disease Outbreak News item or Africa CDC epidemic intelligence report before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Andes virus hantavirus cluster — MV Hondius travel-linked
item.description: Andes hantavirus cluster linked to a South America–Antarctica expedition cruise aboard MV Hondius. 11 reported cases (9 confirmed, 2 probable) and 3 deaths across multiple countries, with multinational case-finding and post-exposure monitoring continuing.

item.title: [CONCERN] Measles — United States
item.description: U.S. measles monitoring remains active because CDC and PAHO surveillance pages continue to track outbreaks, immunity gaps, and regional elimination concerns. Verify current case totals against the CDC measles cases and outbreaks page before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Mpox — clade I, Africa & travel-linked importations
item.description: Mpox clade I and travel-linked importation monitoring remain operationally significant for clinical recognition, specimen routing, and laboratory clade differentiation. Verify current country-level trends and any importation details against specific Africa CDC, WHO, or CDC updates before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Cholera — sub-Saharan Africa, including Republic of Congo
item.description: Cholera activity across parts of sub-Saharan Africa remains a preparedness concern where WASH disruption, population movement, and healthcare access constraints can amplify outbreaks. Verify current country-specific case and fatality figures against specific WHO or Africa CDC reports before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Chikungunya — global outbreak activity
item.description: Chikungunya surveillance remains active across parts of the Americas and other mosquito-active regions. Verify current country burden and case totals against specific PAHO or WHO updates before publication.

item.title: Google News: U.S. bans green-card holders from returning from Ebola-stricken countries - The Washington Post
item.description: U.S. bans green-card holders from returning from Ebola-stricken countries The Washington Post

item.title: NPR: 40,000 people under evacuation orders after chemical tank leak in Southern California
item.description: About 40,000 people were under evacuation orders and schools shut down Friday in Southern California after a storage tank continued to leak a hazardous chemical that officials said could rupture or explode.

item.title: Google News: Health Alert: Worldwide Caution – Updated Public Health Arrival Restrictions and Enhanced Ebola Screening – May 22, 2026 - nz.usembassy.gov
item.description: Health Alert: Worldwide Caution – Updated Public Health Arrival Restrictions and Enhanced Ebola Screening – May 22, 2026 nz.usembassy.gov

item.title: Google News: CDC says green card holders who were recently in countries where Ebola is spreading can’t reenter U.S. - Politico
item.description: CDC says green card holders who were recently in countries where Ebola is spreading can’t reenter U.S. Politico

item.title: Google News: US Headlines: Hantavirus concerns, Arabia becoming a possible player in Iran war - thepostathens.com
item.description: US Headlines: Hantavirus concerns, Arabia becoming a possible player in Iran war thepostathens.com

item.title: Google News: Ebola Ward in Congo Burns After Residents Turn Against Medics - The New York Times

item.description: Houston's Bush Airport will screen travelers from certain countries amid Ebola outbreak KHOU

item.description: Exclusive | U.S. Pauses Visa Issuance for People Who Have Visited Ebola-Hit Countries WSJ
